metadata.notes: Standalone sequel to Above and Below. 10th anniversary edition by Ryan Laukat. New mechanics: ghosts, banish, dangerous caves, pearls, boats, adventure cards.

subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Boards & Books
- 4 player boards (each with ready area, exhausted area, injured area, sell slot, and advancement track)
- 1 reputation board (central area with reputation track, villager display, cider slot, and building rows)
- 1 encounter book (numbered paragraphs with branching choices)
- 1 rulebook

#### Cards
- Starting house cards (1 per player)
- House cards (purchased during the game, placed above ground)
- Outpost cards (placed on cave cards, below ground)
- Key house cards (6 special buildings with key symbol)
- Star house cards (6 special buildings with star symbol and bonus VP conditions)
- Cave cards (two types: normal and dangerous — dangerous cards have a distinct border)
- 40 adventure cards (unique rewards gained from cave exploration)

#### Tokens & Dice
- Wooden ghost tokens (placed on buildings to haunt them)
- Boat tokens (used as free actions for die manipulation or coins)
- Pearl tokens (special goods from dangerous caves — count as highest placed good at endgame)
- Goods tokens: fruit, fish, mushroom (common); rope, clay pot, paper (uncommon); ore, amethyst (rare)
- Coins (1s, 5s, 10s)
- Potion tokens
- Cider tokens
- 7 dice
- 4 wooden cubes (player colors for reputation track)
- 1 round marker
- 1 first-player card

#### Villagers
- 12 starting villagers (3 per player, house on back)
- 18+ regular villagers (face-down pile, displayed on reputation board)
- Special villagers (unique abilities, gained through encounters)

subtopics.title: Game Structure & Core Loop
subtopics.content:
#### Game Structure
Above and Below: Haunted is played over exactly 7 rounds (8 rounds with the longer game variant). Each round, players take turns assigning villagers from their ready area to perform one action per turn until all players pass.

#### Turn Flow
On your turn, you may first perform any number of free actions, then choose 1 of 5 main actions:

1. **Explore** — Send 2+ villagers into a cave for a storybook encounter. You always keep the cave card, even if you fail. Dangerous caves require more lanterns but give bigger rewards including pearls.
2. **Build** — Use a hammer villager to buy a house or outpost card. Houses go above ground, outposts go on cave cards below ground.
3. **Harvest** — Exhaust villagers to collect goods from buildings and/or gain coins. This combines the old harvest and labor actions.
4. **Recruit** — Use a quill villager to hire a new villager from the reputation board. Cheaper villagers (bottom 3 slots) come with a ghost attached.
5. **Banish** — Use a quill villager to remove all ghosts from your buildings, then place one ghost on an opponent's building. Gain rewards based on the targeted building type.

#### Free Actions (before your main action)
- Place a good for sale in your sell area
- Buy a good from another player (minimum 3 coins)
- Pay 1 coin to refresh a building row (discard and redraw 4 cards)
- Use a boat token to auto-roll a six on one die OR gain 1 coin

#### End of Round
1. Refresh villagers using beds, potions, and cider
2. Adjust reputation based on ghost presence and card effects
3. Collect income based on advancement track position
4. Slide villagers left on reputation board and refill empty slots
5. Replenish cider token
6. Pass first-player card

#### Player Interaction
Direct interaction comes from: banishing ghosts onto opponents' buildings, competing for villagers on the reputation board, buying/selling goods between players, competing for buildings in the shared display, and competing for reputation track position.

subtopics.title: Detailed Rules
subtopics.content:
#### Explore Action
Requires at least 2 villagers. Draw the top cave card (normal or dangerous) and place it beside your board. Slide 2+ villagers from ready onto the cave card. Roll 1 die to determine which encounter paragraph to read from the encounter book. The player to your left reads the paragraph aloud, including the bold choices and their explore numbers, but NOT the rewards in parentheses. Choose one option. Roll 1 die per villager on the cave card — each villager's token shows what rolls produce lanterns. Total all lanterns. If insufficient, you may exert villagers: move them to the injured area for 1 extra lantern each. If your total meets or exceeds the explore number, you succeed and gain the listed rewards. Beating the requirement by 2+ often grants bonus rewards. Even if you fail, you keep the cave card. If the cave was dangerous, you also gain a pearl on success.

#### Ghost Mechanic
Ghosts are wooden tokens placed on your building cards. Each ghost on your buildings lowers your reputation at the end of each round. Ghosts arrive through: recruiting cheap villagers (bottom 3 slots on reputation board), certain encounter results, and opponents banishing ghosts onto your buildings. Buildings can only have one ghost each.

#### Banish Action
Requires a quill villager and at least one ghost on your buildings. Exhaust the quill villager. Remove ALL ghosts from your buildings. Take one of the removed ghosts and place it on any opponent's building that does not already have a ghost. You gain a reward based on the type of building you targeted (house, outpost, key house, or star house). Remaining removed ghosts return to the supply.

#### Build Action
Requires a hammer villager. Exhaust the hammer villager and pay the coin cost on the card. Houses and key/star houses go in a row next to your starting house (above ground). Outposts must be placed on an empty cave card you own (below ground). Refill the display after building. Hammer-plus villagers pay 1 fewer coin.

#### Harvest Action
Exhaust one or more villagers. For each villager exhausted, take 1 goods token from one of your buildings OR gain 1 coin. This combines the original game's harvest and labor into a single flexible action. The first player to harvest each round also gains the cider token.

#### Recruit Action
Requires a quill villager. Exhaust the quill villager and pay the coin cost shown on the reputation board for any available villager. The new villager goes to your exhausted area. If you recruit from the 3 cheapest slots, you also gain a ghost on one of your buildings. Quill-plus villagers pay 1 fewer coin.

#### Boats
Boat tokens are gained through encounters, building effects, and adventure cards. As a free action on your turn, you may spend a boat token to either auto-roll a six on one die during an explore action or gain 1 coin. Boats provide crucial die mitigation for risky cave explorations.

#### Adventure Cards
There are 40 unique adventure cards in the game. They are gained as specific rewards from cave exploration encounters. Adventure cards provide various benefits: immediate rewards, ongoing abilities, or end-game victory points. Some adventure cards combo with each other or with specific building types.

#### Pearls
Pearls are a special token gained from successfully exploring dangerous caves. At the end of the game, each pearl counts as your highest-value placed good on the advancement track. If your highest good slot is worth 4 VP per good, each pearl is also worth 4 VP.

#### Advancement Track
The row of circles at the bottom of your player board. Place goods left to right with no gaps. Stacking a second good of the same type goes in the same slot. Each slot shows two numbers: left = VP per good at game end, right = your income level. Once placed, goods cannot be removed. Pearls do not go on the track — they are scored separately based on the track's highest value.

#### Rest Phase
Spend a potion to move 1 villager from injured to exhausted. Spend cider to move 1 villager from exhausted to ready. Use beds on your buildings: each bed moves 1 villager from injured to exhausted OR exhausted to ready (not both steps for same villager).

subtopics.title: End of Game & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Game End
The game ends after exactly 7 rounds (or 8 rounds in the longer game variant).

#### Victory Point Sources
1. **Advancement Track** — Each good on the track is worth the VP value shown above its slot. Goods stacked in the same slot each earn that amount.
2. **Pearls** — Each pearl counts as your highest-value placed good on the advancement track.
3. **Buildings** — Each building you own (starting house, houses, key houses, star houses, outposts) is worth 1 VP. Empty cave cards without outposts do NOT count.
4. **Reputation Bonus** — Most reputation earns 5 VP, second most earns 3 VP, third most earns 2 VP. In a 2-player game, only the leader gets a bonus (3 VP). Ties: add tied positions' rewards, divide by number tied, round up.
5. **Star Building Bonuses** — Some star buildings show conditional VP at the bottom (e.g., 2 VP per potion, 1 VP per cave card).
6. **Adventure Card Points** — Some adventure cards provide end-game VP based on conditions met.
7. **Reputation Track Position** — Check for VP bonus/penalty symbols at your cube's position.

#### Tiebreaker
Most coins remaining wins. Still tied: most villagers. Still tied: most buildings.

#### Important Notes
- Coins are NOT worth VP — they only break ties
- Unused goods not on the advancement track are NOT worth VP unless a card bonus specifically counts them
- Ghosts remaining on buildings at game end do NOT directly cost VP, but they affected reputation throughout the game

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Ghost Management
Ghosts lower your reputation each round, so don't let them pile up. But don't banish too eagerly either — the banish action costs you a quill villager and a turn. Tolerate 1 ghost for a round or two if you're busy building your engine. Banish when you have 2+ ghosts or when sending a ghost to an opponent's star building gives you a juicy reward.

#### Explore Early and Often
Cave cards are free to keep even when you fail, and exploring gives you outpost slots, goods, coins, reputation, and adventure cards. Send 3 villagers instead of 2 when possible — the extra die dramatically increases your chances of hitting higher explore numbers for better rewards.

#### Dangerous Caves Are Worth the Risk
Dangerous caves need more lanterns, but the rewards are bigger and you get a pearl on success. Pearls count as your highest-value good at endgame — if your track goes up to 4 VP per good, each pearl is worth 4 VP. Save your boat tokens for dangerous cave rolls to guarantee sixes.

#### Diversify Your Goods
The advancement track rewards variety — each new good type opens a new slot, increasing both income and per-good VP value. Don't stack 5 of the same good in slot 1 (worth 0 VP each). Spread across 4-5 different types for maximum income and scoring.

#### Boats Are Your Best Friend
Boats provide die mitigation or emergency coins. Use them on dangerous cave explorations to guarantee lanterns, or cash them in when you're 1 coin short of a key building. Don't hoard them — their value diminishes as the game progresses.

#### Don't Ignore Beds
Villagers stuck in injured or exhausted can't work. Buy at least one building with bed icons early. Without recovery, you'll run out of usable villagers by round 4-5.

#### Watch the 7-Round Clock
You only get about 15-20 total actions across the entire game. Don't spend rounds 1-3 purely exploring. Balance exploration with building infrastructure — you need houses and outposts generating goods and income by mid-game.

#### Recruiting Strategy
Cheap villagers come with ghosts, but more villagers means more actions per round. Early game, the ghost cost is worth it for the extra workforce. Late game, pay more for ghost-free recruits to protect your reputation bonus.

rulings.ruling: Unlike the original game, in Haunted you always keep the cave card after exploring, even if you fail the encounter. Failed cave cards still provide outpost slots for future building.
Each building can only have one ghost token on it. When banishing, you cannot place a ghost on an opponent's building that already has a ghost.
You must have at least one ghost on your buildings to take the banish action. You remove ALL ghosts from your buildings, then place exactly one on an opponent's unghostted building.

Pearls count as your highest-value placed good on the advancement track at endgame. If your highest occupied slot shows 4 VP per good, each pearl is worth 4 VP. If you have no goods on the track, pearls are worth 0 VP.
Boats are a free action — using one does not consume your main action for the turn. You can either auto-roll a six on one die during exploration or gain 1 coin.

When recruiting from the 3 cheapest villager slots on the reputation board, you must place a ghost on one of your buildings that does not already have a ghost. If all your buildings already have ghosts, you cannot recruit from those slots.

The harvest action in Haunted combines the old harvest and labor actions. For each villager exhausted, you can take 1 good from a building OR gain 1 coin. You can mix and match across multiple villagers in the same harvest action.
Dangerous cave cards have a distinct visual border that differentiates them from normal caves. You see which type you drew before committing villagers to the exploration.
